body: PT Panca Global Kapital TBK's share portion was reduced by 15.84% to approximately 16.34% after selling 14.03 million shares, while other notable transactions included PT Arthakencana Rayatama's acquisition of 2.59 million shares of PT Akr Corporindo TBK and PT Buana Graha Utama's purchase of 416,400 shares of PT Multi Indocitra TBK, increasing its controlling share portion to 45.6%. These transactions resulted in changes to the ownership percentages of various companies, with Ruhong Holding Pte Ltd remaining the largest holder of PT Bino shares at 88.83%.
title: Multiple Indonesian companies see changes in share ownership and trading activity among investors.

body: Foreign investors sold a net 386.84 million shares on Wednesday, with significant sales in companies such as PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia TBK (-95.42 million shares) and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia TBK (-87.28 million shares). Meanwhile, notable purchases were made in companies like PT Buana Lintas Lautan TBK (43.96 million shares) and PT Maharaksa Biru Energi TBK (41.43 million shares), with a total foreign buying volume of 2.34 billion shares.
title: Foreign Investors Sell 386.84 Million Shares in Indonesian Market on Wednesday

body: PT Gunawan Dianjaya Steel Tbk aims to achieve IDR 2.5 trillion in sales by the end of 2024, with a net profit margin of 5%, driven by maintaining export sales in the Southeast Asia market. As of July 2024, the company has realized IDR 1.7 trillion in sales, with a profit before taxes of IDR 99 billion and EBITDA of IDR 136.6 billion, with domestic market sales being the primary contributor.
title: PT Gunawan Dianjaya Steel targets IDR 2.5 trillion in sales by end of 2024, driven by export sales in Southeast Asia.

body: Nippon Shokubai Indonesia, a petrochemical company, is investing IDR 1.69 trillion in a factory expansion in Cilegon, marking its fourth phase of expansion. The company, which has been operating in Indonesia since 1996, is the sole manufacturer of acrylic acid, acrylic esters, and superabsorbent polymer in the country.
title: Nippon Shokubai Indonesia to Invest IDR 1.69 Trillion in Factory Expansion in Cilegon.

body: PT Dima Investindo will acquire 55% of PT Miki Ojisan Indomitra, a subsidiary of PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDs), with a total of 440 million shares to be acquired, resulting in a change in KMDs' shareholder structure. KMDs reported a revenue increase to IDR 169.98 billion in June 2024, up from IDR 157.99 billion in June 2023, with gross profit rising to IDR 55.47 billion and net profit soaring to IDR 25 billion.
title: PT Dima Investindo to Acquire 55% Stake in PT Miki Ojisan Indomitra from PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa TBK.

body: The Jakarta Stock Price Composite Index (JCI) closed at 7,798, maintaining its bullish trend and breaking another record within the past week, with other indices such as IDX30 and IDX80 also rising by 0.23% and 0.05%, respectively. Blue-chip stocks including PT Bank Central Asia, PT Telkom Indonesia, and PT Unilever Indonesia saw increases, while others like PT Bank Mandiri and PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur were corrected.
title: Jakarta Composite Index Holds Steady at 7,798 with Mixed Performance from Blue-Chip Stocks

body: PT Fersindo Nusa Jaya increased its stake in PT Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk by 6.82% after acquiring 2.84 million shares, and also raised its stake in PT Maharaksa Biru Eneri Tbk to 35.20 million shares. Meanwhile, several other investors made notable transactions, including Zurich Assets International Ltd selling 17.25 million shares, and PT Arthakencana Rayatama acquiring over 8.40 million shares of PT Akr Corporindo Tbk and PT Indocement Tunggal Prakarsa Tbk.
title: Multiple investors in Indonesian companies adjust their stakes through share purchases and sales.

body: PT Suryamas Dutamakmur Tbk, a real estate management developer, reported a net profit of IDR 109.76 billion in H1 2024, a 3.4 times increase from IDR 25.15 billion in H1 2023, driven by a 122% year-on-year revenue growth to IDR 452.34 billion. The company's revenue was primarily generated from sales of land, houses, and apartments, with total assets and cash equivalents reaching IDR 3.51 trillion and IDR 353.12 billion, respectively, as of June 30, 2024.
title: Suryamas Dutamakmur's Net Profit Soars 3.4 Times to IDR 109.76 Billion in First Half 2024

body: Muhammadiyah, a major religious organization in Indonesia, has established two companies to manage mining concessions granted by the Indonesian government, with one serving as a holding company and the other as an operating company. The operating company will be staffed by experts in the mining field and will partner with universities and contractors, with mining activities set to begin after a business plan is devised.
title: Muhammadiyah Central Board Establishes Two Companies to Manage Indonesian Government-Granted Mining Concessions.

body: PT Eastparc Hotel Tbk will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 5.15 billion for fiscal year 2024, a 30.55% decrease from IDR 7.42 billion in FY 2023, with a net profit of IDR 15.42 billion and total equity of IDR 461.28 billion. The company will distribute IDR 1.25 per share on October 3, 2024, with cum-ex dividend dates set for September 23-24 and recording date for shareholders on September 25.
title: PT Eastparc Hotel Tbk to Distribute IDR 5.15 Billion Interim Dividend for Fiscal Year 2024

body: PT Lautan Luas Tbk, a chemical distributor, has implemented sustainability strategies through its subsidiaries, resulting in a 5.7% year-on-year revenue growth to IDR 3.7 trillion in the first half of 2024, driven mainly by domestic sales. The company's net profit also surged 166% year-on-year to IDR 136.56 billion, with 90% of its revenue coming from domestic sales, particularly in the B2B segment of food, beverages, chemicals, and personal care industries.
title: PT Lautan Luas Tbk implements sustainability strategies through solar panels and green warehouse construction.

body: PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten Tbk is planning a rights issue of 5.18 billion Series C shares at IDR 50 per share, aiming to raise IDR 114.10 billion to expand its business by acquiring building assets from the Banten Regional Government. The proceeds will improve the bank's capital structure and liquidity, reducing interest expenses and leading to positive changes in its profit, with the bank's total authorized capital standing at IDR 8 trillion and issued paid-up capital at IDR 4.30 trillion.
title: PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten to Raise IDR 114.10 Billion Through Rights Issue of 5.18 Billion Series C Shares.
